verses.text: Nebuchadnezzar dreams, his spirit troubled, and summons the magicians to tell it. A restless king. God can disturb the proudest heart with a single dream.
Nebuchadnezzar dreams, his spirit troubled, and summons the magicians to tell it. A restless king. God can disturb the proudest heart with a single dream.
Nebuchadnezzar dreams, his spirit troubled, and summons the magicians to tell it. A restless king. God can disturb the proudest heart with a single dream.
He demands they declare both dream and meaning, on pain of death. An impossible test. He exposes the emptiness of all who claim to know the future.
He demands they declare both dream and meaning, on pain of death. An impossible test. He exposes the emptiness of all who claim to know the future.
He demands they declare both dream and meaning, on pain of death. An impossible test. He exposes the emptiness of all who claim to know the future.
They beg him to tell the dream; he charges them with stalling, the matter being gone from him. Cornered pretenders. False wisdom is shown helpless when truly tried.
They beg him to tell the dream; he charges them with stalling, the matter being gone from him. Cornered pretenders. False wisdom is shown helpless when truly tried.
They beg him to tell the dream; he charges them with stalling, the matter being gone from him. Cornered pretenders. False wisdom is shown helpless when truly tried.
They confess no man on earth can do it, only 'the gods, whose dwelling is not with flesh.' An unwitting truth. They point, despite themselves, to the God who does dwell with men.
They confess no man on earth can do it, only 'the gods, whose dwelling is not with flesh.' An unwitting truth. They point, despite themselves, to the God who does dwell with men.
Enraged, the king orders all the wise men slain, and they seek Daniel too. Innocent endangered. The folly of the proud sweeps the godly into peril.
Enraged, the king orders all the wise men slain, and they seek Daniel too. Innocent endangered. The folly of the proud sweeps the godly into peril.
Daniel answers with wisdom and prudence, and asks the king for time to show the interpretation. Calm faith. He neither panics nor presumes, but seeks room to seek God.
Daniel answers with wisdom and prudence, and asks the king for time to show the interpretation. Calm faith. He neither panics nor presumes, but seeks room to seek God.
Daniel answers with wisdom and prudence, and asks the king for time to show the interpretation. Calm faith. He neither panics nor presumes, but seeks room to seek God.
He bids his friends seek 'mercies of the God of heaven,' and the secret is revealed in a night vision. Prayer prevails. United supplication brings down the answer from heaven.
He bids his friends seek 'mercies of the God of heaven,' and the secret is revealed in a night vision. Prayer prevails. United supplication brings down the answer from heaven.
He bids his friends seek 'mercies of the God of heaven,' and the secret is revealed in a night vision. Prayer prevails. United supplication brings down the answer from heaven.
Daniel blesses God, who 'changeth the times and the seasons... removeth kings, and setteth up kings,' and reveals deep things. Praise first. Before he uses the gift, he worships the Giver.
Daniel blesses God, who 'changeth the times and the seasons... removeth kings, and setteth up kings,' and reveals deep things. Praise first. Before he uses the gift, he worships the Giver.
Daniel blesses God, who 'changeth the times and the seasons... removeth kings, and setteth up kings,' and reveals deep things. Praise first. Before he uses the gift, he worships the Giver.
Daniel blesses God, who 'changeth the times and the seasons... removeth kings, and setteth up kings,' and reveals deep things. Praise first. Before he uses the gift, he worships the Giver.
He goes to Arioch and is brought before the king to make known the interpretation. The way opens. God's timing turns a death-sentence into an audience.
He goes to Arioch and is brought before the king to make known the interpretation. The way opens. God's timing turns a death-sentence into an audience.
Daniel disclaims all human power: 'there is a God in heaven that revealeth secrets,' and makes known what shall be. Glory to God. He takes no credit, pointing all to the Revealer.
Daniel disclaims all human power: 'there is a God in heaven that revealeth secrets,' and makes known what shall be. Glory to God. He takes no credit, pointing all to the Revealer.
Daniel disclaims all human power: 'there is a God in heaven that revealeth secrets,' and makes known what shall be. Glory to God. He takes no credit, pointing all to the Revealer.
The dream concerns things to come, revealed not for Daniel's wisdom but for the king's sake. Humble honesty. God's servant claims nothing for himself.
The dream concerns things to come, revealed not for Daniel's wisdom but for the king's sake. Humble honesty. God's servant claims nothing for himself.

The king saw a great image — gold, silver, brass, iron, and iron mixed with clay — struck by 'a stone... cut out without hands' that became a mountain filling the earth. A vision of ages. Earthly empires give way to a kingdom not of man's making.

The king saw a great image — gold, silver, brass, iron, and iron mixed with clay — struck by 'a stone... cut out without hands' that became a mountain filling the earth. A vision of ages. Earthly empires give way to a kingdom not of man's making.

The king saw a great image — gold, silver, brass, iron, and iron mixed with clay — struck by 'a stone... cut out without hands' that became a mountain filling the earth. A vision of ages. Earthly empires give way to a kingdom not of man's making.

The king saw a great image — gold, silver, brass, iron, and iron mixed with clay — struck by 'a stone... cut out without hands' that became a mountain filling the earth. A vision of ages. Earthly empires give way to a kingdom not of man's making.

The king saw a great image — gold, silver, brass, iron, and iron mixed with clay — struck by 'a stone... cut out without hands' that became a mountain filling the earth. A vision of ages. Earthly empires give way to a kingdom not of man's making.
Daniel says, 'Thou, O king, art... head of gold,' given dominion by the God of heaven. Greatness acknowledged. Yet even his glory is a gift from above.
Daniel says, 'Thou, O king, art... head of gold,' given dominion by the God of heaven. Greatness acknowledged. Yet even his glory is a gift from above.
Daniel says, 'Thou, O king, art... head of gold,' given dominion by the God of heaven. Greatness acknowledged. Yet even his glory is a gift from above.
After him shall rise a lesser kingdom, then a third of brass, then a fourth strong as iron, breaking all. Empires in succession. History unfolds exactly as God foretold.
After him shall rise a lesser kingdom, then a third of brass, then a fourth strong as iron, breaking all. Empires in succession. History unfolds exactly as God foretold.
The feet of iron and clay are a divided kingdom, part strong, part brittle, that will not cleave together. Weakness in strength. No human empire holds together for ever.
The feet of iron and clay are a divided kingdom, part strong, part brittle, that will not cleave together. Weakness in strength. No human empire holds together for ever.
The feet of iron and clay are a divided kingdom, part strong, part brittle, that will not cleave together. Weakness in strength. No human empire holds together for ever.

'In the days of these kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom, which shall never be destroyed' — the stone cut without hands. Christ's reign. His kingdom outlasts and shatters all the empires of men.

'In the days of these kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom, which shall never be destroyed' — the stone cut without hands. Christ's reign. His kingdom outlasts and shatters all the empires of men.
The king falls before Daniel, makes him ruler of Babylon and chief of the wise, and Daniel sets his friends over the province. Faith exalted. God lifts up those who honour Him before kings.
The king falls before Daniel, makes him ruler of Babylon and chief of the wise, and Daniel sets his friends over the province. Faith exalted. God lifts up those who honour Him before kings.
